title: JWT Authentication Middleware Config
description: Configuration for the JWT authentication middleware plugin

issuer.description: Expected issuer (iss claim). If set, tokens must match.

audience.description: Expected audience (aud claim). Strongly recommended: when unset, a token minted for any relying party at the same issuer is accepted (confused-deputy risk on shared IdPs). When set, tokens must match.

clock_skew_seconds.description: Clock skew tolerance in seconds for exp/nbf validation

skip_signature_validation.description: Skip signature validation. TEST ONLY: ignored by the compiled plugin, so it cannot disable verification in production.

jwks_url.description: JWKS URL. Not handled by jwt-auth; use the oidc-auth plugin for JWKS-based verification.

groups_claim.description: Claim name to extract consumer groups from (e.g. 'roles', 'groups'). Value is set as x-auth-consumer-groups (comma-separated).

public_key_pem.description: Inline public key in PEM format. Not handled by jwt-auth; supply public_key_jwk instead, or use oidc-auth.

public_key_jwk.description: Inline public key as a JWK, used to verify the token signature (RSA RS256/384/512, EC ES256/384). When set, every token must carry a valid signature under this key.
